summary:
Levosimendan is not indicated nor supported by any available evidence as a treatment for amyotrophic lateral sclerosis. Current standard therapies for ALS include riluzole and edaravone; there are no studies showing efficacy of levosimendan in this condition.  

(Note: This summary focuses only on clinically relevant facts about ALS therapy.)

The response correctly identifies that levosimendan lacks support in existing literature regarding ALS treatment. It accurately notes established ALS therapeutics while explicitly stating absence of effectiveness data for levosimendan. Both statements directly address core aspects of therapeutic validity within neurodegenerative disease management context without introducing unsupported claims. 

Final answer: $\boxed{\text{No}}$ Levo-simendan is ineffective for treating Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis due to lack of supporting clinical trials or mechanisms applicable to motor neuron degeneration seen in ALS.
